dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7951 IH: Long-Term Good Neighbor Authority Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Valadao (for himself and Mr. Panetta) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources, and in addition to the Committee on Agriculture, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the Agricultural Act of 2014 and the EXPLORE Act to provide for long-term Good Neighbor Authority, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Long-Term Good Neighbor Authority Act.

paragraph.text: Section 8206(a) of the Agricultural Act of 2014 (16 U.S.C. 2113a(a)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1)(B), by striking Governor and inserting Governor, Indian tribe,;
in paragraph (5), by striking Governor and inserting Governor, Indian tribe,; and
in paragraph (6), by striking or Indian tribe.

paragraph.text: Section 8206(b) of the Agricultural Act of 2014 (16 U.S.C. 2113a(b)(1)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1), by amending subparagraph (A) to read as follows:
The Secretary may enter into a good neighbor agreement with a Governor, Indian tribe, or county—

clause.text: to carry out authorized restoration services in accordance with this section; and
for a term not to exceed 20 years.

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (3), by striking Governor and inserting Governor, Indian tribe,.

paragraph.text: Section 8206(b)(2)(C)(i) of the Agricultural Act of 2014 (16 U.S.C. 2113a(b)(2)(C)(i)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subclause (I), by striking on; and

clause.text: by striking clause (i) and inserting subclause (I); and
in item (bb), by striking the Good Neighbor Authority for Recreation Act and inserting section 351 of the EXPLORE Act (16 U.S.C. 8571).

subsection.text: Section 351(b)(1) of the EXPLORE Act (16 U.S.C. 8571(b)(1)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking county and inserting county—; and
by striking to carry out authorized recreation services in accordance with this title. and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: to carry out authorized recreation services in accordance with this title; and
for a term not to exceed 20 years.
